name: cathode
description: Loud, technical, emissive — phosphor on dark glass, one live wire of cyan; inkwell's opposite number, measured to WCAG AAA. Nine themes: the glass/daylight pair plus seven channels (terminal, blueprint, ledger, manila, litmus, sonar, redline).

themes.name: glass
themes.description: Phosphor on dark glass — the default broadcast.

themes.name: daylight
themes.description: The away kit — full brightness, measured in daylight.

themes.name: terminal
themes.description: Green phosphor on black glass — the original console.

themes.name: blueprint
themes.description: Cyan-washed drafting paper, deep azure lines.

themes.name: ledger
themes.description: Pale ledger green, ruled and balanced — accounting stock.

themes.name: manila
themes.description: Folder-stock amber, warm and procedural — paperwork weather.

themes.name: litmus
themes.description: Test-strip pink, acid-checked — diagnosis in daylight.

themes.name: sonar
themes.description: Cyan depth, green ping — instruments below the waterline.

themes.name: redline
themes.description: Soot chassis, klaxon signal — running hot on purpose.

brief.name: cathode
brief.intent: Phosphor on dark glass, one live wire of cyan — where inkwell absorbs light, cathode emits it. A color system for agentic products that ship signal, and can prove it.

cases.question: Does this system read as loud, technical phosphor firing at dark glass?
cases.note: emission, not absorption — built to be noticed and still be trusted

cases.question: Do the status hues read as indicator lamps — lit only when the machine has something to report?
cases.note: state is a lamp: pulse, flare and klaxon report, never decorate

cases.question: Does this system read as quiet, warm, paper-like editorial print?
cases.note: that is inkwell — the opposite number

source: Converted one-shot from the Cathode POC (cathode.tokens.json v1.2, design-system-poc, 2026-07-02): `cathode.` namespace stripped, refs rewritten {cathode.color.*} -> {color.*}, semantic.<theme> split into semantic-<theme>.tokens.json and semantic.solid into semantic-shared.tokens.json; per-token $type kept verbatim; primitives untouched. Version bumped 1.2.0 -> 1.2.1 for the AAA patch: re-points (one step deeper on the same ramp) against the full plan-014 contract matrix — glass accent.subtle-bg volt.900 -> volt.950 (6.36 -> 8.54); daylight accent.default volt.800 -> volt.900 (5.48 on subtle-bg / 6.68 on raised -> 8.07 / 9.84) and accent.hover volt.900 -> volt.950 (keeps hover distinct from the new default); terminal accent.subtle-bg pulse.900 -> pulse.950 (6.38 -> 9.35) and text.muted graphite.400 -> graphite.300 (6.20 on raised -> 7.95; muted now equals secondary); blueprint accent.subtle-bg volt.300 -> volt.200 (6.36 -> 8.07); ledger accent.subtle-bg pulse.300 -> pulse.200 (6.38 -> 7.63); manila accent.subtle-bg flare.300 -> flare.200 (6.56 -> 8.15); litmus text.muted graphite.800 -> graphite.850 (6.64 on raised -> 8.22; muted now equals secondary); sonar accent.subtle-bg pulse.900 -> pulse.950 (6.38 -> 9.35); redline accent.default klaxon.300 -> klaxon.200 (4.55 on subtle-bg / 5.99 on raised -> 8.34 on raised), accent.hover klaxon.200 -> klaxon.100, accent.subtle-bg klaxon.800 -> klaxon.900 (6.34 vs the new default -> 8.34). No hex edits.
